initial_request_score.label: partially_supported

evidence.quote: In this article, we provide a compact and comprehensive survey on zero-shot learning.
Zero-shot learning is a learning paradigm that tries to develop a recognition model to recognize mutually exclusive training and testing classes.

issues.claim: Labeled training images of seen classes and semantic information...
issues.reason: This describes the input to a ZSL model, not the input to the survey paper (which is literature/papers). The paper states 'we comprehensively reviewed more than 100 papers'.

issues.claim: Classification accuracy on unseen object classes
issues.reason: This is the goal of ZSL models, not the survey paper. The paper's goal is to 'present performance evaluation measures' and 'provide a survey'.

initial_request_score.fix_suggestion: Change `input_data` to 'Literature on zero-shot learning (100+ papers)' and `quantifiable_goal` to 'Comprehensive categorization and review of ZSL methods and datasets'.

evidence.quote: Semantic spaces are usually categorized as (i) engineered semantic spaces, and (ii) learned semantic spaces
91. Wang, W... A survey of zero-shot learning... (2019).

step_scores.label: mostly_supported

evidence.quote: all ZSL methods fall in one of the four categories: (i)... A_ZSL... (ii)... T_ZSL... (iii)... Lt_ZSL... (iv)... I_ZSL

performance can be measured by calculating top-1 accuracy... top-T (where T=5, 10...)

all ZSL methods fall in one of the four categories
In this article, we have provided a compact and comprehensive survey on ZSL methods

issues.reason: This specific technique name is not mentioned in the text; it is an inference based on the paper's claims of comprehensiveness.
